title: madhuca kompongsonensis
intro: Madhuca kompongsonensis, also known as the Cambodian butter tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is a large deciduous tree native to Cambodia, known for its edible seeds that are rich in nutrients and possess medicinal properties. The seeds of Madhuca kompongsonensis have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin infections, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The seeds can be prepared into herbal remedies such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support overall health and well-being, and are also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
